meaning: n.拘泥形式,(艺术或宗教上的)形式主义,虚礼
examples: This is what is called formalism.
He is a devoted fan of western music and hates the formalism of Mishima.
Therefore, there is no formalism for what these use dependencies mean yet.
In his eyes, Mr Schnittke was in thrall to formalism, cosmopolitanism and, worse still, religion.
With greater formalism, you can set a plan to drive tactical value with the execution of the social business method.
